title: gymnospermium altaicum subsp. odessanum
intro: Gymnospermium altaicum subsp. odessanum, also known as the Odessa club moss, is a rare subspecies of the Gymnospermium altaicum plant, belonging to the coniferous family, Pinaceae. This perennial plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Russian folk medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, gout, and kidney stones. The plant's extracts and essential oils are known to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing blood pressure, improving kidney function, and alleviating symptoms of arthritis. In herbal preparations, Gymnospermium altaicum subsp. odessanum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
